pending.body: finding_summary: `record_task_run_start` still allows a failed historical project to reopen the same bead while another project already has that bead active. In `src/adapters/fs.rs:5168-5218`, the finalized-failed path calls `reopen_failed_exact_attempt(...)` and returns before the new cross-project duplicate check at `src/adapters/fs.rs:5277-5283` runs. A concrete case is: project A fails on bead X, project B is now running bead X, then `run resume` on project A succeeds and creates a second active lineage for bead X. Because milestone reconciliation collapses to the latest run per bead (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:967-999`), that parallel-active state can be hidden from progress accounting instead of surfacing as corruption. affected_files: [`src/adapters/fs.rs`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs`]. remediation_hint: reject `reopen_failed_exact_attempt` whenever `running_attempts_for_bead` contains another project, or move the same-bead active-project guard ahead of all finalized-attempt reopen paths.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 2 -> Plan and Implement
